problem: I have a drawer with 6 forks, 6 spoons, and 6 knives in it. If I reach in and randomly remove three pieces of silverware, what is the probability that I get one fork, one spoon, and one knife?

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of pieces of silverware in the drawer?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many pieces of silverware do we need to randomly remove?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the total number of ways to choose 3 pieces of silverware out of 18?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose one fork out of the 6 available forks?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose one spoon out of the 6 available spoons?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we choose one knife out of the 6 available knives?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we find the total number of ways to choose one fork, one spoon, and one knife?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the numerator of the probability fraction?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How can we express the probability of choosing one fork, one spoon, and one knife?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Can we simplify the fraction to its lowest terms?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final probability that we get one fork, one spoon, and one knife when randomly removing 3 pieces of silverware?
classified_questions.label: b
